project: OpenSearch SQL - Remove Datetime UDT from Core
description: Remove EXPR_DATE, EXPR_TIME, EXPR_TIMESTAMP UDTs from core/ module. All datetime types use standard Calcite SqlTypeName.DATE/TIME/TIMESTAMP. Fix value boundary (fromObjectValue, valueForCalcite), cast handling, OpenSearch adapter, replace 17 UDFs with standard operators, and clean up UDT infrastructure.

userStories.title: Override TIMESTAMP precision to 9 in OpenSearchTypeSystem
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Calcite type system to support TIMESTAMP(9) so that nanosecond precision can be declared in schemas.
acceptanceCriteria: Return 9 for TIMESTAMP and TIMESTAMP_WITH_LOCAL_TIME_ZONE, delegate to super for all others
userStories.notes: Follow the Flink pattern: FlinkTypeSystem overrides getMaxPrecision to return 9 for TIMESTAMP types.

userStories.title: Change PPLReturnTypes to return standard Calcite types
userStories.description: As a developer, I want PPL datetime return type inferences to produce standard Calcite DATE/TIME/TIMESTAMP instead of UDTs.
acceptanceCriteria: Also fix TIME_APPLY_RETURN_TYPE to return standard TIME/TIMESTAMP instead of UDTs
userStories.notes: This will cause many downstream failures. Fix compilation errors in this story, leave test failures for later stories.

userStories.title: Change OpenSearchTypeFactory.convertExprTypeToRelDataType to standard types
userStories.description: As a developer, I want ExprCoreType.DATE/TIME/TIMESTAMP to map to standard Calcite types instead of UDTs.
acceptanceCriteria: In OpenSearchTypeFactory.convertExprTypeToRelDataType(), change DATE/TIME/TIMESTAMP cases to return createSqlType(SqlTypeName.DATE/TIME/TIMESTAMP) instead of createUDT()
userStories.notes: This changes the schema types for OpenSearch tables. Combined with US-002, all datetime types in the plan become standard.

userStories.title: Fix ExprValueUtils.fromObjectValue to handle int/long datetime inputs
userStories.description: As a developer, I want fromObjectValue(Object, ExprType) to handle native Calcite datetime representations (int for DATE/TIME, long for TIMESTAMP) in addition to String.
acceptanceCriteria: TIMESTAMP case: handle Long (epoch millis via Instant.ofEpochMilli) and Instant, fall through to String
DATE case: handle Integer (epoch days via LocalDate.ofEpochDay), fall through to String
TIME case: handle Integer (millis of day via LocalTime.ofNanoOfDay(int * 1_000_000L)), fall through to String
Existing String path must remain unchanged
userStories.notes: This is additive — existing String path unchanged. The new paths handle native Calcite representations.

userStories.title: Fix ExprValue.valueForCalcite() to return native Calcite representations
userStories.description: As a developer, I want datetime ExprValue classes to return int/long from valueForCalcite() instead of String, matching standard Calcite expectations.
acceptanceCriteria: Override valueForCalcite() in ExprDateValue: return (int) date.toEpochDay()
Override valueForCalcite() in ExprTimestampValue: return timestamp.toEpochMilli()
userStories.notes: This is the most impactful change — every place that calls valueForCalcite() on datetime values now gets int/long instead of String. Many tests will break. Fix compilation errors here, leave test assertion failures for later.

userStories.title: Fix ExtendedRexBuilder.makeCast for datetime types
userStories.description: As a developer, I want CAST to datetime types to use standard Calcite CAST instead of dispatching to PPL UDF calls.
acceptanceCriteria: In core/src/main/java/org/opensearch/sql/calcite/ExtendedRexBuilder.java, remove the EXPR_DATE/EXPR_TIME/EXPR_TIMESTAMP cases from the UDT switch in makeCast()
Standard Calcite CAST handles DATE/TIME/TIMESTAMP natively via super.makeCast()
Keep EXPR_IP and EXPR_BINARY cases unchanged
userStories.notes: After US-003, convertExprTypeToRelDataType returns standard types, so visitCast produces standard types. makeCast no longer needs to dispatch datetime to UDFs.

userStories.title: Fix OpenSearch adapter: enumerator, pushdown analyzers, serialization
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the OpenSearch adapter to work with standard Calcite datetime types instead of UDTs.
acceptanceCriteria: OpenSearchIndexEnumerator: valueForCalcite() now returns int/long — verify the enumerator still works (it provides data to Calcite, new types match standard expectations)
PredicateAnalyzer: change any ExprUDT.EXPR_TIMESTAMP checks to SqlTypeName.TIMESTAMP checks
AggregateAnalyzer: verify convertRelDataTypeToExprType still works (it has dual-path, standard path should handle it)
ExtendedRelJson: verify serialization works without UDT type info (standard types serialize natively)
SpanFunction: change ExprUDT switch to SqlTypeName checks for DATE/TIME/TIMESTAMP
WidthBucketFunction: change dateRelatedType() to check SqlTypeName instead of ExprUDT
userStories.notes: This is the highest-risk story. The OpenSearch adapter touches pushdown, serialization, and data flow. Fix one file at a time.

userStories.title: Replace 17 PPL UDFs with standard/library operators in PPLFuncImpTable
userStories.description: As a developer, I want PPL functions with standard SQL equivalents to be registered as SqlStdOperatorTable/SqlLibraryOperators operators instead of custom UDFs.
acceptanceCriteria: In PPLFuncImpTable.populate(), change datetime registrations for: YEAR, QUARTER, MONTH, DAY/DAYOFMONTH/DAY_OF_MONTH, DAY_OF_WEEK/DAYOFWEEK, DAY_OF_YEAR/DAYOFYEAR, HOUR/HOUR_OF_DAY, MINUTE/MINUTE_OF_HOUR, SECOND/SECOND_OF_MINUTE, CURRENT_DATE/CURDATE, CURRENT_TIME/CURTIME, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP/NOW/LOCALTIMESTAMP, LAST_DAY, DAYNAME, MONTHNAME
Use SqlStdOperatorTable equivalents for standard SQL functions
Use SqlLibraryOperators.DAYNAME and SqlLibraryOperators.MONTHNAME for MySQL library functions
Do NOT replace: WEEK (semantic mismatch), EXTRACT (operand type mismatch), TIMESTAMPADD/TIMESTAMPDIFF (name+operand mismatch), LOCALTIME (type mismatch)
userStories.notes: Already implemented and committed (8de0cabca). All 17 functions were already registered with standard operators. Build/tests/spotless all pass.

userStories.title: Remove datetime UDT infrastructure from core/
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to clean up the now-unused datetime UDT classes and constants.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove ExprUDT.EXPR_DATE, EXPR_TIME, EXPR_TIMESTAMP enum values (keep EXPR_BINARY, EXPR_IP)
Remove OpenSearchTypeFactory.createUDT() cases for datetime types
Remove OpenSearchTypeFactory.leastRestrictive() UDT preservation logic for datetime
Clean up isTimeBasedType() to only check standard SqlTypeName (remove UDT checks)
userStories.notes: Only remove datetime UDTs. EXPR_BINARY and EXPR_IP must remain. Search for all references before removing.

userStories.title: Remove DatetimeUdtRewriter and UdfOperandAdapter from api/ module
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to remove the post-planning rewrite that is no longer needed since UDTs are gone from core/.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove the rewrite call from UnifiedQueryPlanner.plan()
Remove the adapt call from UnifiedQueryCompiler.compile()
Update or remove DatetimeUdtRewriterTest — tests should work without the rewriter since core/ now produces standard types
userStories.notes: Files never existed on this branch (ralph/datetime-udt-rewrite-v2). DatetimeUdtRewriter and UdfOperandAdapter were created on a different branch. No references exist in the codebase. N/A.

userStories.title: Fix unit test failures across all modules
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all unit tests to pass after the UDT removal.
acceptanceCriteria: Run ./gradlew test and fix all failures
Expected failure areas: tests asserting UDT type names, tests comparing String datetime values, tests checking ExprUDT enum values
Do NOT delete tests — fix assertions to match new behavior
userStories.notes: All unit tests already pass after prior stories fixed compilation and assertion issues. No additional fixes needed.

userStories.title: Run Calcite integration tests and document breakage
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to run all CalciteXXXIT integration tests and document what breaks.
acceptanceCriteria: Document all failures in progress.txt with: test name, error message, likely cause
Do NOT fix integration test failures in this story — just document them
userStories.notes: Integration tests ran successfully (cluster spun up). 12 test classes had failures, ~250 total test failures documented in progress.txt. Major categories: (1) 500 errors from datetime functions on null/standard types, (2) plan assertion mismatches from UDT→standard type changes, (3) CAST precision loss, (4) standalone datetime function failures.
